CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, value VARCHAR);
SELECT value[:4] FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1;
SELECT value[-6:] FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2;
SELECT value[:] FROM test_strings WHERE id = 3;
SELECT value[-3:] FROM test_strings WHERE id = 4;
SELECT id, value[:2] || '...' AS shortened_value FROM test_strings;
SELECT (SELECT value[:2] FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1) || (SELECT value[3:] FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2) AS combined_value;
CREATE TABLE additional_strings (id INT, additional_value VARCHAR);
SELECT ts.id, ts.value[:3] AS slice_value, as.value[:2] AS additional_slice FROM test_strings ts JOIN additional_strings as ON ts.id = as.id;
SELECT id, value[-5:-2] FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (1, 2);
SELECT value[:5] || ' - ' || value[-5:] AS concatenated FROM test_strings;
CREATE VIEW sliced_view AS SELECT value[:3] AS short_value FROM test_strings;
SELECT short_value FROM sliced_view WHERE short_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT value[:] FROM (SELECT value FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1) AS temp_table;
SELECT (SELECT value[:4] FROM test_strings) as sliced1, (SELECT value[-4:] FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2) as sliced2;
